description: There are plenty of things to think about when considering who to get to help you with your number one asset be it a farm life style block or other. We are a family owned and operated fencing contractor company that is based in the Bay of Plenty and our company motto is "There is nothing more important to us than satisfied clients" . We have years of fencing and in fact farming experience so we can work with you if you are unsure of what will work best for your property or business. We are detail orientated and offer excellent workmanship backed up by our two year guarantee. We carry liability insurance and meet all relevant health and safety requirements. No job is too big or too small and we build every fence to the same high standards that we would expect if it were our own. A tidy well constructed fence which has been well thought out and executed will not only look good and keeping stock where you want them but it will also add value to your investment. We specialise in all types of rural fencing; post and batten, post and rail, post and netting, deer fencing, electric fencing, equine fencing, repair fencing, security fencing, wetland protection, stock yards, loading ramps and more. We can offer a full service to our customers where we can provide materials, labour and machinery to build or repair your fences or provide the labour only to construct the full fence or even ram in the posts and strainers for you to finish in your own time.

title: Southern Humates
excerpt: Put simply, Southern Humates is an accumulation of partially decayed forest and other carbon-rich plant residues that have been preserved and compressed. Many …
description: Put simply, Southern Humates is an accumulation of partially decayed forest and other carbon-rich plant residues that have been preserved and compressed. Many people associate Humates with ground up coal however, Humates are actually found in the layer above referred to as lignite which is softer, brown coal which has a higher moisture content than black coal. Natural Humates, such as those provided by Southern Humates, are widely regarded as a premier source of organic matter and are thus recogonised as the most effective of all soil conditioners due to their high exchange capacity and excellent water retention capacity. Humates, Humic Acid

description: AJ Auto Electrical are the experts you need when dealing with today's motor vehicles. Established in 2003, AJ Auto Electrical have years of experience dealing with the more complex side of your vehicle's insides. There's a lot to be said for the modern technology underpinning today's motor vehicles. Not only have innovations such as ABS braking, electronic stability control and airbags made the driving experience immeasurably safer, but technology has also led to positive advances in areas such as fuel efficiency and emission control. However, the explosion of automotive technology can occasionally come at a price. Where once your average Joe could claim to have sufficient engineering know-how to fix most vehicle problems, today's electronics frequently make that an impossibility. That's why you need the help of the experts on hand when it comes to all matters auto electrical. AJ Auto Electrical have a team of friendly, experienced staff, purpose built premises, a huge stock of parts in store, and a fleet of mobile vehicles so they can come to you.

description: Challenge was formed in 1998 in an initiative by Fletchers who saw an opportunity in the New Zealand fuel supply industry to create an independent retailer network. The goal was to create a network that could compete with the big four corporate fuel entities.With an emphasis on service that is built on the independence and unique nature of our owner-operated stations, and because it is New Zealand owned, Challenge quickly became a force in the industry offering matching fuel services with a local community feel. When Fletchers sold the Challenge network to Caltex in 2003, the network remained independent under the Challenge banner. In 2010 Challenge signed an agreement with fuel distributor Farmlands Fuel (a division of Farmlands Co-operative Society Limited) for it to become the sole supplier of fuel to the Challenge network. The co-operative has a membership of over 60,000 shareholders, most of whom are farmers who can purchase fuel using their card. With Farmlands as its partner, the Challenge network continues to grow year on year in New Zealand.

title: Auto Trail Limited
excerpt: Auto Trail has been a family business, 100% New Zealand owned and operated since 1987. Originally, the business operated out of a small garage and a rep vehicl…
description: Auto Trail has been a family business, 100% New Zealand owned and operated since 1987. Originally, the business operated out of a small garage and a rep vehicle, providing automotive accessories to Automotive workshops, garages and service stations. The company name (Auto for automotive and Trail for trailer components) was chosen to reflect our business offerings and our range widened from initially accessories only to include automotive parts, tools, trailer components, tractor spares and workshop/garage equipment. At the same time, our target market increased to include workshops, engineering businesses, farming contractors and similar. Trade business sales make up a large proportion of our business although we have always had a retail offering for those customers wanting to do their own vehicle servicing and trailer repairs. The launch of our full e-commerce website in 2018 serves as a means for Auto Trail to reach customers outside of the Waikato and offers the ability for customers to see some of the range of products we have available. In time the website product range will increase to include Trailer parts and other tool brands.

In 1990 R.C.Schroder Ltd purchased Lin's Auto Centre (founded in the late 1980's in Masterton by the late Lin Morris). This acquisition added Auto Parts and Accessories, Hoses, Adhesives and Sealants, Motor Oils and Tools to the product range. In 2001 the organisation purchased the assets of Masterton stockist Brakes and Spares Ltd, adding a comprehensive range of Brake and Clutch components. The Wellington Trade and Retail store situated in Tory Street was sold in April 2009 and closed by the new owner three years later. Schrodoco still operate a small distribution warehouse in Wellington supplying wholesalers and manufacturers only. Schrodoco and Lin's Auto Centre list more than 35,000 inventory items. description: Marlborough's first Ford dealer, Edward Parker, a general trader, took on the franchise about 1913.In 1920. Dix and Sons obtained the franchise and re-located to dedicated motor garage premises. However when they took on Chevrolet in 1927, they lost the Ford franchise which went to the Lucas Brothers. Ed Lucas, and his brother Fred, who had started a service garage based in Seddon in 1919. In 1929, Lucas Brothers confirmed their commitment to the motorists of Marlborough and constructed a purpose built Dealership in Alfred Street. Blenheim (Which remained for over 60 years) When Ed Lucas retired in 1958, his son Ted took over the Blenheim business, which he ran for a further 33 years. Ted Lucas remembers the day he made his biggest ever sale, to Montana Wines in 1973. He delivered 26 new Ford Tractors together with a variety of plough's, cultivators, loaders and water tankers. How could he forget a massive sale like that! In 1998, Colonial Motor Company purchased the Ford Franchise in its existing location on Nelson Street Blenheim and operated till 31 March 2010 when it was sold to respected local vehicle dealer Paul McKendry.

His name was Alan Bradfield White, hence the name Bradfield Farm Limited which was established in 1966. In assisting to developing the flat swampy farm, Pop chose maize. But when he had problems finding a reliable contractor to harvest his crop, he decided to purchase his own. That was Bradfields first combine harvester. With the farm established, Pop and Kevin turned their skills to manufacturing and exporting spray units to Australia. As a teenager Kevin helped out in the workshop. Kevin slowly started to build the contracting division up, while June and Graeme enjoyed sailing to the islands trips abroad. Like his Dad, Kevin had the need for a digger. So he a mate Ed Kay went halves. It was the first purchase of many under and the company Rural Contractors Limited was born. By the time Kevin and Kirsten married in the early 2000's the fleet had grown on the machinery side and the services Bradfields could provide.

The Carrfields team knows the rural industry, is proud of our history and is an advocates for the local farming community. We draw on a diverse range of skills, strengths, experience and leading edge knowledge to help our clients. We believe in the power of team and value the contribution - with our enterprising spirit and diverse skills our aim is to build prosperity and increase productivity in our rural communities. Carrfields employs passionate kiwis from the Far North to Invercargill. Our diverse team lives and works in rural and urban New Zealand, servicing our clients with a comprehensive suite of products including on-farm advisory, farm supplies, insurance, livestock and wool marketing options.

description: Central Harvest Ltd is a company that offers a pit silage service covering maize, cereal and grass silage. The company is run in conjunction with two other partners. For more information go to Central Harvest Ltd. The aim of the company is to help farmers increase their profit margins by producing good quality silage. Like so many agricultural contractors before them, the McCarthy brothers of Ladbrooks near Christchurch used to look upon their business as a stepping-stone to farming, but the enterprise has grown into something more substantial than many farms. Founded about 20 years ago, McCarthy Contracting Ltd keeps ten loyal staff busy year-round and employs up to 25 in the harvest season. It all started when Robert who was working for contractors in his local district spotted an opening for round baling. Robert ran the business on his own for two years while his brother Grant, who was then working as a diesel mechanic, helped out at evenings and weekends but rapid growth saw it develop into a dynamic business with year-round work.

excerpt: Chris' father had always done a small but of contracting and after his semi-retirement he continued this venture and at its peak he would run two tractors and …
description: Chris' father had always done a small but of contracting and after his semi-retirement he continued this venture and at its peak he would run two tractors and conventional ploughs and anything up to 5 combine harvesters. Chris worked for Agrifarm Machinery and Landpower Ashburton (now Claas Harvest Centre) when he left school but always enjoyed helping his father out on weekends and when time allowed. Chris then attended University in Auckland but would always be home during the holidays and summer break to drive a tractor or combine. After traveling to Ireland to work for a harvest he returned to Methven to work at Wright Farms and the opportunity arose to secure a contract ploughing there if he purchased a reversible plough, this combined with his fathers clients was enough to take the plunge. Chris Woods Contracting was formed in early 2013 with the purchase of a 5 furrow Kverneland reversible plough. Chris is always looking at new cultivation options and believes his business growth is due to having a small operation that focuses on quality. He has since updated to a Kverneland 7 furrow semi mounted plough to cope with demand.
address: 71 Barkers Road, Methven, 7730, New Zealand

description: Rob Clark of Clark Acreage is a licensed building practitioner offering an array of building services Auckland wide. Clark Acreage is a Kaukapakapa based building and landscaping company and carries out work throughout the greater Auckland area from Wellsford to south of the Bombay Hills. If you're looking for expert certified builders you can have confidence in, call Clark Acreage. Make sure both you and your home are always treated with care and respect by a licensed building practitioner. Rob Clark of Clark Acreage completed his building apprenticeship while in the army. Since then he has worked either as a self-employed builder or sub-contracted to other building companies and has worked on literally hundreds of properties throughout Auckland and, more recently, Christchurch. This vast experience has given Rob a huge range of building skills and in-depth knowledge of building processes. If it's made of wood, Rob can put it together.
